segments.text: Be silent before me, you islands! Let the nations renew their strength! Let them come forward and speak; let us meet together at the place of judgment.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who has stirred up one from the east, calling him in righteousness to his service? He hands nations over to him and subdues kings before him. He turns them to dust with his sword, to windblown chaff with his bow.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He pursues them and moves on unscathed, by a path his feet have not traveled before.
Who has done this and carried it through, calling forth the generations from the beginning? I, the Lord —with the first of them and with the last—I am he.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The islands have seen it and fear; the ends of the earth tremble. They approach and come forward;
Be strong!
segments.speaker: Companions

segments.text: The metalworker encourages the goldsmith, and the one who smooths with the hammer spurs on the one who strikes the anvil. One says of the welding, 'It is good.' The other nails down the idol so it will not topple.
But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, you descendants of Abraham my friend,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I took you from the ends of the earth, from its farthest corners I called you. I said, 'You are my servant'; I have chosen you and have not rejected you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So do not fear, for I am with you; do not be dismayed, for I am your God. I will strengthen you and help you; I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: All who rage against you will surely be ashamed and disgraced; those who oppose you will be as nothing and perish.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Though you search for your enemies, you will not find them. Those who wage war against you will be as nothing at all.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For I am the Lord your God who takes hold of your right hand and says to you, Do not fear; I will help you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Do not be afraid, you worm Jacob, little Israel, do not fear, for I myself will help you,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: declares the Lord, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
See, I will make you into a threshing sledge, new and sharp, with many teeth. You will thresh the mountains and crush them, and reduce the hills to chaff.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: You will winnow them, the wind will pick them up, and a gale will blow them away. But you will rejoice in the Lord and glory in the Holy One of Israel.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The poor and needy search for water, but there is none; their tongues are parched with thirst. But I the Lord will answer them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will make rivers flow on barren heights, and springs within the valleys. I will turn the desert into pools of water, and the parched ground into springs.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I will put in the desert the cedar and the acacia, the myrtle and the olive. I will set junipers in the wasteland, the fir and the cypress together,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: so that people may see and know, may consider and understand, that the hand of the Lord has done this, that the Holy One of Israel has created it.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Present your case,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: says the Lord.
Set forth your arguments,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: says Jacob's King.
Tell us, you idols, what is going to happen. Tell us what the former things were, so that we may consider them and know their final outcome. Or declare to us the things to come,
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: tell us what the future holds, so we may know that you are gods. Do something, whether good or bad, so that we will be dismayed and filled with fear.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: But you are less than nothing and your works are utterly worthless; whoever chooses you is detestable.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I have stirred up one from the north, and he comes— one from the rising sun who calls on my name. He treads on rulers as if they were mortar, as if he were a potter treading the clay.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Who told of this from the beginning, so we could know, or beforehand, so we could say, 'He was right'? No one told of this, no one foretold it, no one heard any words from you.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I was the first to tell Zion, 'Look, here they are!' I gave to Jerusalem a messenger of good news.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: I look but there is no one— no one among the gods to give counsel, no one to give answer when I ask them.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: See, they are all false! Their deeds amount to nothing; their images are but wind and confusion.
segments.speaker: God
